caveats: a: The BLS occupation data are for the broader fabricated-metal subsector.
a: Current automation intensity varies between high-speed beverage and food-can plants and smaller specialty-can producers.
rho: Manufacturing-wide AI surveys overrepresent larger organizations relative to the target lens.
rho: Some apparent AI gains are conventional controls, machine vision, or robotics rather than AI substitution.
e: The frozen firm count is margin-bridged and may misclassify firms in a concentrated industry.
e: Silgan's greater-than-half US food-can share is a large-company market signal, not a direct count of eligible LMM firms.
s5: Gallup is cross-industry and measures plans, not completed qualifying transfers.
s5: Plant acquisitions by strategic buyers may not represent acquisition of an independent lens firm.
q: Silgan is the dominant US metal-food-can producer and may face different bargaining dynamics than specialty LMM suppliers.
q: Contract terms vary by can type, customer, decoration, tooling, and plant dedication.
d5: Public-company figures are global or multi-region and can include market-share changes.
d5: Unit volume is not a constant-quality measure because can sizes, gauges, decoration, and end types change.
o: Alternative packaging can eliminate metal-can units even when packaged-product demand persists.
o: Consolidation can move production to a different operator without eliminating the need for an operator.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: High-volume cans and ends are supplied repeatedly under annual or multi-year agreements, frequently from plants near customer filling operations; specialty and general-line cans may use shorter quoted runs, but revenue still depends on recurring packaging demand and customer qualification.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive can plants, shells, non-control financing vehicles, firms outside the EBITDA band, pure distributors, and producers whose qualifying can manufacturing operation is not independently transferable.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying metal cans, lids, and ends on recurring or repeat contracts to external food, beverage, pet-food, aerosol, and general-line customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures occupations for the broader NAICS 332 fabricated-metals subsector, not wage-weighted, not-yet-automated tasks in 332431. I apply lower exposure than the broader mix because can production is already high-volume and mechanically automated, leaving AI-relevant work mainly in planning, maintenance analytics, quality inspection, scheduling, procurement, customer service, and administration. This may understate exposure at smaller specialty plants with more manual changeovers and inspection.
caveats: The BLS occupation data are for the broader fabricated-metal subsector.
Current automation intensity varies between high-speed beverage and food-can plants and smaller specialty-can producers.
a.change_evidence: A 332431 occupational and wage matrix combined with plant time studies separating automated line tending, maintenance, quality, changeover, warehousing, planning, sales, and administration would materially refine exposure.
a.rationale: Continuous forming, coating, printing, conveying, and end-making lines already automate much direct handling. Remaining AI opportunity lies in vision inspection, predictive maintenance, production scheduling, yield and scrap analysis, specification management, demand planning, and indirect office work rather than replacing most line labor.

d5.bridge: Silgan and Crown are large global manufacturers rather than US LMM lens firms. Silgan reported approximately 3% higher metal-container unit volume in 2025 and expects 2026 improvement led by pet food; Crown reports recent beverage-can demand growth. I temper these nominal, company-specific signals into modest five-year constant-price quantity growth because food cans face substitution and harvest volatility and small firms may not share beverage scale growth. The proxy can overstate growth if leaders gain share or understate niche local and specialty demand.
caveats: Public-company figures are global or multi-region and can include market-share changes.
Unit volume is not a constant-quality measure because can sizes, gauges, decoration, and end types change.
d5.change_evidence: Can Manufacturers Institute shipment data and six-digit constant-dollar Census shipments split by beverage, food, pet food, aerosol, and general-line cans, with customer packaging forecasts, would materially change the year-five ratio.
d5.rationale: Recurring food, beverage, pet-food, and general-line packaging volumes support durability. Beverage and pet-food cans provide growth, sustainability and shelf-life attributes help metal, while plastic, paper, glass, composites, lightweighting, consumer mix, deposit rules, crop variability, and overcapacity constrain the outlook.

caveats: The frozen firm count is margin-bridged and may misclassify firms in a concentrated industry.
Silgan's greater-than-half US food-can share is a large-company market signal, not a direct count of eligible LMM firms.
e.change_evidence: A verified review of all 19 dataset-estimated LMM firms covering ownership, captive status, segment revenue, customer concentration, plant assets, certifications, and independence from a larger packaging group would materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: The target population is small and the mainstream food and beverage can market is highly concentrated, capital-intensive, and often tied to large strategic suppliers or captive customer plants. Some specialty, aerosol, and general-line can producers can remain independent, external-customer businesses with transferable teams and customer qualifications.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 332431 ($1,949,229,389) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 332431 ($22,792,627,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $1,055,507 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 332431); class <5: 19 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,462,053 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $286,855 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 6 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,780,667 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,918,967 -> IN $1-10M band; class 10-19: 6 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,803,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,334,749 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 7 firms, avg revenue/firm $42,023,714 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,245,053 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $114,679,667 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $22,500,151 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,016,145,857 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $199,367,817 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 19

caveats: Alternative packaging can eliminate metal-can units even when packaged-product demand persists.
Consolidation can move production to a different operator without eliminating the need for an operator.
o.change_evidence: Customer packaging roadmaps measuring insourcing, refill systems, material substitution, digital printing, contract-packaging integration, and procurement shifts to mega-suppliers would materially change operator requirement.
o.rationale: Customers still require physical cans and ends produced on qualified equipment near filling plants, with accountable food-contact, sealing, coating, decoration, and quality performance. Software can optimize the factory but cannot replace the manufacturer for the current service basket within five years.

q.bridge: Silgan is much larger than the lens firms, but its disclosure that about 90% of projected 2026 metal-container sales are under multi-year arrangements and that agreements pass through metal and often other manufacturing costs reveals the industry's contracting logic. I infer that incumbents can retain some operational gains between negotiation cycles, while formulas, renewals, concentrated buyers, and competitive benchmarking share much of the benefit. The proxy may understate retention for differentiated specialty cans with shorter runs and fewer suppliers.
caveats: Silgan is the dominant US metal-food-can producer and may face different bargaining dynamics than specialty LMM suppliers.
Contract terms vary by can type, customer, decoration, tooling, and plant dedication.
q.change_evidence: LMM supply agreements and renewal histories showing productivity clauses, open-book rights, cost formulas, price resets, volume commitments, rebates, and margins before and after process improvements would materially change retention.
q.rationale: Long contracts and local plant relationships protect volume and can delay price pressure, but sophisticated packaged-goods customers, formula pricing, productivity commitments, rebids, and scale competition limit five-year retention. The effect is stronger in commoditized cans than in specialty formats or decoration.

caveats: Manufacturing-wide AI surveys overrepresent larger organizations relative to the target lens.
Some apparent AI gains are conventional controls, machine vision, or robotics rather than AI substitution.
rho.change_evidence: Plant-level deployment studies from LMM can makers showing scaled use, integration cost, false-reject rates, unplanned downtime, scrap, labor hours, and customer acceptance would materially move the range.
rho.rationale: Structured, repetitive production creates favorable conditions for AI-assisted inspection, maintenance, scheduling, and yield optimization. Five-year implementation is constrained by legacy controls, integration cost, scarce downtime, food-contact and customer quality requirements, cybersecurity, and smaller firms' limited data teams.

s5.bridge: Gallup reports five-year sale or transfer intentions for US employer-business owners across industries, with 22% planning such an event. I reduce the base for this capital-intensive, strategically concentrated niche because qualifying LMM independents are scarce and some transitions will be asset sales, closures, or integrations rather than transferable going-concern control deals. The proxy may understate opportunistic strategic acquisitions.
caveats: Gallup is cross-industry and measures plans, not completed qualifying transfers.
Plant acquisitions by strategic buyers may not represent acquisition of an independent lens firm.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of completed private metal-can transactions and plant sales from PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, packaging advisers, and trade publications, matched to a verified eligible-firm denominator, would materially change this probability.
s5.rationale: Aging-owner succession creates potential transactions, but the tiny estimated population, customer qualification, dedicated assets, environmental diligence, and buyer concentration reduce the likelihood that an eligible firm completes a qualifying transfer in any five-year window.

principal_driver: Recurring high-volume customer demand and local supply relationships make the operating basket highly durable.
principal_weakness: A tiny, concentrated target universe and already-automated production leave limited addressable labor and strong customer bargaining power.

sources.supports: Defines NAICS 332431 as establishments manufacturing metal cans, lids, and ends.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: Metal Can Manufacturing

sources.supports: Provides broader-subsector occupation evidence, including large employment in assemblers, welders, machinists, press operators, and production supervisors.
sources.title: Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing: NAICS 332

sources.supports: Identifies AI manufacturing applications and the data, integration, explainability, reliability, and safety barriers to industrial deployment.
sources.title: 2026 Roadmap on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Smart Manufacturing

sources.supports: Reports only 18% of surveyed manufacturers have a formal operational AI strategy, with data availability, format, skills, culture, and legacy systems cited as barriers.
sources.title: Shaping the AI-Powered Factory of the Future

sources.supports: Reports greater-than-half US metal-food-can share, about 90% of projected 2026 metal-container sales under multi-year arrangements, metal-cost pass-through, a 300-mile general sales radius, 3% 2025 unit growth, and competition from other packaging materials.
sources.title: Silgan Holdings Inc. 2025 Form 10-K

sources.supports: Gallup reports that 22% of US employer-business owners planned a sale, transfer, or public offering within five years and 52.3% of employer businesses are owned by people age 55 or older.
sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan

sources.supports: Reports recent global beverage-can demand growth, demand drivers including new products and sustainability, and risks from overcapacity, customer loss, packaging preferences, regulation, and metal supply.
sources.title: Crown Holdings, Inc. 2025 Form 10-K

title: Metal Can Manufacturing
